title: Layered LLM Defense: A Biologically-Inspired Defense-in-Depth Architecture for Large Language Models
description:
Layered LLM Defense (LLD) is a four-layer defense-in-depth framework for Large Language Models combining formal verification, antifragile learning, information-theoretic interface hardening, and moving target defense, with a set of biologically-inspired architectural primitives (Hormesis, Immune Memory, Microbiome, Self-Adversarial Loop with thymus selection, Fever mode, Herd Immunity).

Live-LLM validation. 100 attack vectors against Llama-3.3-70B via Groq: raw ASR 7.0%, refusal-corrected ASR 0–2%, FPR 0.0% (measured on a 40-input clean baseline, likely optimistic). Mean latency 2.22 ms (~100x faster than LLM inference itself).

Ablation study on 510-vector simulated benchmark (HarmBench markers + GCG/AutoDAN/PAIR adversarial-suffix patterns): Vanilla configuration 89.8% ASR, Bio-Off configuration 71.2% ASR. The Vanilla → Bio-Off gain of 18.6 percentage points is the most transferable claim. The further drop to ~0% in the Full configuration is partly a simulation artifact: the Microbiome whitelist easily flags abstract markers that do not appear in any clean baseline.

No "perfect defense" claim. Honest open issues: simulation artifacts in the ablation, small live-LLM sample (100 vectors), 0.0% FPR likely optimistic, no head-to-head LlamaGuard run yet (adapter ready), 2 borderline bypass cases await human verdict, no human red team, single author, not peer reviewed. All limitations documented in the report.

Open source under Apache 2.0. Standard-library Python, 453 deterministic unit tests (no LLM calls, no network calls), no third-party dependencies for the core. The author is actively looking for collaborators to run real-LLM benchmarks at scale, perform independent red-teaming, and evaluate against production traffic.
notes: This is a working research artifact, not a peer-reviewed publication. Reviewers and replication efforts are explicitly welcomed. The defense pipeline is implemented in Python standard library only with no third-party dependencies.
